title: Parallel Architectures: UMA, NUMA and Message-Passing
summary: Covers multiprocessor system types (UMA/SMP, NUMA/DSM, message-passing), scalability challenges, and memory system classifications.
content_points: UMA/SMP: shared memory, equal access time for all processors. Simple but does not scale.
NUMA/DSM: physically distributed memory, shared address space, non-uniform access time.
Message-passing: each processor has local memory; communication via explicit messages.
Scalability: linear speedup is ideal but unachievable; limited by sequential program portions (Amdahl's law) and communication overhead.
Very large processor counts may cause performance to plateau or drop.
learning_objectives: Describe UMA, NUMA, and message-passing multiprocessor systems
Explain scalability challenges in large parallel systems
Apply Amdahl's law in the context of parallel computing
